data.title: Autohome's Q1 Non-GAAP Earnings, Revenue Decline
data.description:
Autohome reported Q1 non-GAAP earnings Thursday of 3.52 Chinese renminbi ($0.49) per diluted American depositary share, down from 4.07 renminbi a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 3.18 renminbi.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31 was 1.45 billion renminbi, compared with 1.61 billion renminbi a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 1.44 billion renminbi.

Shares of Autohome were 3% higher in recent premarket activity Thursday.

data.title: Bharat Forge Q4 Consol Profit 2.83 Bln Rupees
data.description:
Bharat Forge Ltd :

  • Q4 CONSOL PROFIT 2.83 BILLION RUPEES

  • Q4 CONSOL REVENUE FROM OPERATIONS 38.53 BILLION RUPEES

data.title: Warmer Weather Lifts UK Retailer Next plc's Fiscal First Quarter Sales
data.description:
Next plc raised its guidance for fiscal 2026 after first-quarter full-price sales outpaced expectations on the back of a weather-driven increase in summer clothing sales.

The British retail group on Thursday booked an 11.4% year-over-year jump in full-price sales for the 13 weeks ended April 26, beating its 6.5% growth forecast for the period by 55 million pounds sterling. The boost was largely attributed to warmer weather, which benefited the sale of summer-weight clothing.

"Sales in our Retail shops have been much stronger than we expected but, in our experience, shops benefit disproportionately from the favourable weather. So we are expecting our Retail sales to return to being broadly flat for the rest of the year," the group said in a trading update.

Analysts at Bernstein hailed Next's first quarter as another "beat and raise" performance, with "strong numbers across the board," led by the group's international and UK segments.

Next's online international and total UK full-price sales rose 29.6% and 7.3%, respectively, on a yearly basis, while retail sales increased 5.2%. Total product full-price sales gained 12.1%.

The company noted that some of the sales in the first quarter may have been pulled forward from the second quarter. Consequently, its sales guidance for the second quarter and the remainder of the year remains unchanged. The group's forecast for total group sales for the full fiscal year was maintained at 6.6 billion pounds.

Meanwhile, Next upgraded its full-price sales guidance for the fiscal year to 5.4 billion pounds from 5.3 billion pounds. It now expects 1.08 billion pounds in group profit before tax, up from the previous 1.07 billion pounds.

Shares were up nearly 1% as of late Thursday morning trade.

data.title: YSB Buys Back HK$2 Million of Shares; Shareholders to Propose Another Repurchase Mandate
data.description:
YSB repurchased 300,000 shares for HK$2.1 million at an average of HK$7.0147 apiece, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The company will cancel the repurchased shares, which were bought back under a share repurchase mandate approved in 2024.

Meanwhile, the company will allocate HK$100 million under the 2024 share repurchase plan up until the May 31 annual general meeting for 2025.

Shareholders will also propose to the board to grant another mandate to buy back up to 10% of the company's total shares under a fresh mandate for 2025.

data.title: Ithaca Energy Target Resumed at 170p by BofA
UK believed to have made concessions on food, agriculture in exchange for lower tariffs
data.description: It is reported that the UK has agreed to give some concessions on food and agriculture imports from the US in exchange for lower tariffs on autos. More to come..

data.title: Coal India Signs MoU to Supply Renewable Power
data.description:
Coal India has signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding with AM Green Ammonia, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The MoU aims to supply renewable power from Coal India's planned 4.5 GW projects to AM Green's upcoming ammonia facility in Kandla or other locations, the filing said.

data.title: US Consulate General in Pakistan's Lahore directs staff to shelter in place
data.description:
The U.S. Consulate General in Lahore, Pakistan, has directed its staff to shelter in place amid reports of drone explosions, downed drones and possible airspace incursions, the country's State Department said on Thursday.

The consulate said it has also received initial reports that authorities may be evacuating some areas adjacent to Lahore’s main airport, according to a statement.

The move comes as Pakistan said on Thursday it shot down 25 drones from India in its airspace while India said it had "neutralised" Pakistan's attempt to engage military targets using drones and missiles, as the latest conflict between the two nuclear-powered neighbours intensified.

data.title: Escorts Kubota Q4 Net Profit From Continuing Operations 2.51 Bln Rupees
data.description:
Escorts Kubota Ltd :

  • DIVIDEND 18 RUPEES PER SHARE

  • Q4 NET PROFIT FROM CONTINUING OPERATIONS 2.51 BILLION RUPEES

  • Q4 REVENUE FROM OPERATIONS 24.3 BILLION RUPEES

data.title: Kenyan shilling little changed, LSEG data shows
data.description:
The Kenyan shilling was little changed against the dollar on Thursday, London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) data showed.

At 0948 GMT the shilling was quoted at 128.90/129.50 to the dollar, compared with 129.00/129.50 at Wednesday's close.

data.title: Thermax Raises Stake in Bioenergy Unit to 76.97%
data.description:
Thermax increased its shareholding in its subsidiary Thermax Bioenergy Solutions Private to 76.97% from 65% following the allotment of 13 million equity shares of the latter to the former at 10 Indian rupees each.

The equity investment aims to boost liquidity, support operations, and meet short-term financial requirements, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Shares of the Indian energy services provider fell more than 2% at market close on the National Stock Exchange on Thursday.

Tel: +44 (0)20 7220 0500

About Bigblu Broadband plc

Bigblu Broadband plc (AIM: BBB.L), is a leading provider of alternative superfast and ultrafast broadband solutions throughout Australasia and the Nordics. BBB delivers a portfolio of superfast and ultrafast wireless broadband products for consumers and businesses unserved or underserved by fibre.

High levels of recurring revenue, increasing economies of scale and Government stimulation of the alternative broadband market in many countries provide a solid foundation for significant organic growth as demand for alternative ultrafast broadband services increases around the world.

BBB's range of solutions includes satellite, next generation fixed wireless and 4G/5G FWA delivering between 30 Mbps and 150 Mbps for consumers, and up to 1 Gbps for businesses. BBB provides customers a full range of services including hardware supply, installation, pre-and post-sale support, billings and collections, whilst offering appropriate tariffs depending on each end user's requirements.

Importantly, as its core technologies evolve, and more affordable capacity is made available, BBB continues to offer ever-increasing speeds and higher data throughputs to satisfy market demands for broadband and broadband services. BBB's alternative broadband offerings present a customer experience that is similar to that offered by wired broadband and the connection can be shared in the normal way with PCs, tablets and smart phones via a normal wired or wireless route

data.title: OM Infra Wins Major UP Jal Nigam Water Supply Project
data.description:
Indian EPC infrastructure developer OM Infra secured a key water supply infrastructure project by Uttar Pradesh Jal Nigam (Urban), Shahjahanpur.

The contract is valued at 1.29 billion Indian rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The project covers survey, soil testing, design, and full construction or repair of an overhead tank, pump house, boundary wall, staff quarters, gate, site development, and pipeline works, the company said.

Shares of OM Infra rose nearly 1% at market close on the National Stock Exchange on Thursday.

data.title: Tutor Perini Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Tutor Perini Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 53 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 30 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 9 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 6 cents to 15 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 18.8% to $1.25 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.06 billion.

  • Tutor Perini Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 53 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $28 million.

  • Tutor Perini Corp shares had risen by 1.9% this quarter and lost 2.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 37.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the construction & engineering peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Tutor Perini Corp is $40.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 09:50 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. (For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com)

data.title: Takeda Pharmaceutical Profit Falls 25% in Fiscal 2024; Tariff Impact is Expected to be Limited
data.description:
Takeda Pharmaceutical reported a 25% decrease in net profit attributable to owners of the parent to 107.9 billion yen for fiscal 2024, from 144.1 billion yen a year ago.

The company's earnings per share (EPS) fell to 67.23 yen in the fiscal year ended March 31, from 92.09 yen a year ago, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

Revenue jumped 7.5% to 4.581 trillion yen from 4.263 trillion yen a year earlier.

For fiscal 2025, the company expects attributable profit of 228 billion yen or 144.81 yen per share, while net sales are expected to reach 4.53 trillion yen.

The company expects to pay interim and year-end dividends of 100.00 yen per share, each, for the current fiscal year. It declared a final dividend of 98.00 yen per share for the fiscal 2025, payable from June 26.

The company said the current forecasts do not reflect the potential impact of tariffs imposed by the US or other countries.

Takeda said its primary and contract manufacturing sites are located in the US, Europe, and Japan, leading to a limited potential exposure to US and China tariffs based on their current revenue and import values.

To further mitigate tariff impacts on its imports, Takeda is implementing measures such as inventory and supply chain management.

data.title: Astral Closes Al-Aziz Plastics Acquisition
data.description:
Astral completed the acquisition of 100% equity shares of Al-Aziz Plastics on May 5, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The company had signed definitive agreements with the existing promoters and shareholders of AL-AZIZ Plastics in April to acquire the stake for 330 million Indian rupees.

data.title: Barclays Lowers Coloplast to Equal-weight on CEO Departure, Guidance Cut
data.description:
Barclays on Wednesday downgraded Coloplast (COLO-B.CO) to equal-weight from overweight after the departure of its chief executive officer and downside revision to guidance.

The Danish medical device company updated its 2025 organic growth guidance to 7% in constant currencies, compared with 8% to 9% previously. With the resignation of CEO Kristian Villumsen, meanwhile, analysts are concerned about "a prolonged period of leadership uncertainty, a reset to mid-term expectations and an unclear strategic direction (particularly around [Interventional Urology]).

Barclays reduced its adjusted EBIT estimates for 2025 to 2027 by 5% to 7% and price target to 750 Danish kroner from 1,010 kroner.

"The September [Capital Markets Day], which will still go ahead, could be an opportunity for some strategic clarity, particularly around the future of the Interventional Urology business," the research firm said. "Management are working on the strategy for this business going forward given recent underperformance, with it being too early to comment on whether a potential divestment is an option."

data.title: Tariffs News: Trump Flags 'Major Trade Deal.' Why a U.K. Breakthrough Matters.
data.description:
By Brian Swint

Investors have been waiting on tenterhooks for weeks to learn how big President Donald Trump's tariffs will be when they finally take effect. They should get more insight today.

Trump said that a "major trade deal" will be announced at 10 a.m. on Thursday, one that will be the "first of many," in a post on the Truth Social social-media platform on Wednesday. The Wall Street Journal reported it will unveil an agreement with the U.K.

While details of the arrangement are still scarce, they're probably not as important for the stock market as the news on whether and how much it will reduce the 10% tariffs imports from the U.K. would have been subject to under Trump's so-called reciprocal tariff plan. The hope is that it will provide a template for further arrangements that can bring overall levies down to minimal levels before the expiration of the pause on the tariff plan on July 8.

Other countries negotiating with the Trump administration will be able to study the arrangement to decide how much is worth conceding in negotiations. In theory, Britain should get the best deal possible.

For one thing, the U.K. has a trade surplus in goods with the U.S. recently, meaning that it buys more goods from America than it sells — one of Trump's goals with tariffs is reducing U.S. trade deficits. Furthermore, the U.K. is only the fifth-biggest purchaser of U.S. exports, behind Canada, Mexico, China, and Japan. And the European Union, which represents 20 countries including Germany and France, will be negotiating as a bloc.

There's a risk that the deal may not change much in practice. Britain would like a reduction in the U.S.'s 25% steel and automobile tariffs, but the 10% baseline tax may remain in place, the Journal reported, citing unnamed people familiar with the talks. In return, the U.K. may offer concessions on a digital tax put on big U.S. technology firms, the paper said.

Still, any reprieve would be likely to help U.K. aerospace companies like Rolls-Royce and BAE Systems. Pharmaceutical company AstraZeneca stands to gain, as does spirits maker Diageo, which owns Johnnie Walker whiskey and Tanqueray gin.

But today, the important point is that trade deals to reduce tariffs are possible. Stocks have largely recovered losses since Trump's April 2 "Liberation Day" announcement of widespread taxes on imports, which they then delayed a week later. In the longer term, investor confidence will only be able to grow if this deal is followed up by more, particularly with the biggest U.S. partners.

Tariffs Keep Fed on Hold

Yesterday's press conference following the Federal Reserve interest-rate decision confirmed that tariffs are the greatest unknown for policymakers. Chair Jerome Powell and colleagues kept rates on hold and said they're waiting before making the next move.

For now, the economy is strong enough to give the Fed time. Markets are pricing in an 80% chance for another decision for unchanged rates in June and a 55% chance of a quarter-point cut in July.

Write to Brian Swint at brian.swint@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
